mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 6% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investor sentiment weakens.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates weak demand conditions, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in demand contraction scenarios are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust supply-side activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate earnings improve.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) creates a 'stagflation' risk, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150k signals strong labor market dynamics, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as corporate hiring optimism drives equity prices.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in strong labor market scenarios are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2% combined with rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL > 2%) indicates a slowing economy with inflationary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105 combined with rising housing starts (HOUST > 1000) signals strong supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in supply expansion scenarios are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
